title: Bound synthetic-edge synthesis: lazy per-lens vs eager union
body: POC v2's edgesForLens() + synthesizeFromField() unified accessor decides at query time whether synthetic edges (auto-derived lens edges from item fields) get computed. Production scale requires bounding the cardinality. Eager union — synthesizeAll(config) computing every lens's synthetic edges per call — is O(L × N) per query; for L=50 lenses and N=1000 items that's 50K synthetic edges per call. Lazy per-lens — only synthesize for the lens being queried — is O(N) per call, but requires the unified-accessor signature to know which lens is being queried. Lazy is correct for production scale; eager simpler to implement. Couples to issue-070 (cache placement) — caching synthetic edges and invalidating on item-field change is the orthogonal optimization. Surfaced as gap #26 in the integration-map review.
